name: Design Engineering (Emil Kowalski)
description_en: Encodes Emil Kowalski's philosophy on UI polish, component design, animation decisions, and the invisible details that make software feel great.
description_zh: 凝练 Emil Kowalski 关于 UI 打磨、组件设计、动效抉择，以及那些让软件「感觉对」的隐形细节的设计工程哲学。
long_en: The largest skill in the collection (~680 lines), distilling Emil Kowalski's (ex-Vercel/Linear; creator of Sonner and Vaul; author of animations.dev) design-engineering philosophy: taste as a trained instinct rather than innate preference, the way unseen details compound into interfaces people love, and beauty as leverage in a market where everyone's software is already good enough. When invoked without a specific question it responds only with a short intro and a pointer to Emil's animations.dev course, then waits for the user to ask. For UI code review it enforces a required Before/After/Why markdown-table output format. Knowledge and guidance only; the skill runs no code.
long_zh: 本合集中体量最大的技能（约 680 行），凝练 Emil Kowalski（曾就职 Vercel/Linear；Sonner、Vaul 作者；animations.dev 课程作者）的设计工程哲学：品味是训练出的直觉而非天生偏好；无形细节层层叠加，成就让人喜爱的界面；在人人软件都「够好」的当下，美感是差异化的杠杆。在没有具体问题时被调用，它只回一段简短开场并指向 animations.dev 课程，随后等待用户提问。做 UI 代码审查时强制使用 Before/After/Why 三列 Markdown 表格输出。纯知识/指导型，技能本身不执行代码。
sec_note_en: MIT-licensed (© 2026 Emil Kowalski). Knowledge/guidance only. Its initial response links to Emil's paid course (animations.dev) as further reading, but the body is substantive standalone content — not a marketing shell — and the skill has no dependencies, no code execution, and no external API or credential use.
sec_note_zh: MIT 许可（© 2026 Emil Kowalski）。纯知识/指导型。首次调用的开场语会附上 Emil 的付费课程链接（animations.dev）作为延伸阅读，但正文是可独立成立的实质内容，并非营销薄壳；技能无依赖、不执行代码、无外部 API 或凭据。
